EVIDENTIARY HEARING HELD. THE FOLLOWING WITNESSES WERE SWORN IN AND TESTIFIED FOR PLAINTIFF: MAHDI ALLAH, CRAIG HAYES, ROBERT NEWMAN AND VALERIE STATHAM. SYLVIA ROSE WAS SWORN IN AND TESTIFIED FOR DEFENDANT. EXHIBITS 1 TO 5 WERE MARKED AND ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE. EVIDENTIARY HEARING RE: CLAIM OF IMPROPER ACTIONS: COURT FINDS DEFENDANT IS IN VIOLATION OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED. EXHIBITS WERE RETURNED. PER DIEM ASSESSED PURSUANT TO G.C. 68086(A)(1) AND G.C. 68086(B)(1). JUDGE: JAMES WARREN REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR #1558 (301)

calendar.matters:
Evidentary Hearing Re: Claim Of Improper Actions
Off Calendar Feb-15-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Francisco G. Torres, Esq., Zanghi, Torres and Arshawsky, (415) 977-0444, for Plaintiff and Moving Party Conard House Inc.
Defendant and Opposing Party Sylvia Rose appeared in Propria Person, (415) 865-9874
RULING:
Evidentiary Hearing held. The following witnesses were sworn in and testified for Plaintiff: Mahdi Allah, Craig Hayes, Robert Newman and Valerie Statham. Sylvia Rose was sworn in and testified for Defendant. Exhibits 1 to 5 were marked and admitted into evidence. Evidentiary hearing Re: Claim of Improper Actions: Court finds defendant is in violation of the settlement agreement. Order and Judgment signed. Exhibits were returned. Per Diem assessed pursuant to G.C. 68086(a)(1) AND G.C. 68086(b)(1). (301/JLW/) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-08-2004 Off Calendar

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-08-2004 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: FRANCISCO G TORRES FOR PLAINTIFF CONARD HOUSE AND VALERIE STRATHAN, MANAGER
DEFENDANT SYLVIA ROSE IN PRO PER

SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE HELD. CASE SETTLED. TERMS PLACED ON RECORD. STIPULATION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT TO REMAIN IN POSSESSION PROVIDED $1539 IS PAID IN MONTHLY INSTALLMENTS. $100 DUE 12/17/04, AND $100 EACH MONTH UNTIL PAID IN FULL AND $39 IS DUE ON 2/1/05; $463 IS DUE ON THE 1ST OF EACH MONTH, NO LATER THAN THE 5TH. DEFENDANT IS TO COMPLY WITH THE TERMS OF THE LEASE; NOT TO SUBLET; NO OFFENSIVE CONDUCT; NO ACTS OF VIOLENCE. MANAGMENT MAY CONDUCT INSPECTIONS OF PROPERTY, UPON 48 HOURS NOTICE. DEFENDANT WAIVES RIGHT TO TRIAL OR STAY OF EXECUTION. IF DEFENDANT COMPLIES TO 2/28/06 PLAINTIFF WILL FILE A DISMISSAL WITH PREJUDICE. IF DEFENDANT BREACHES AGREEMENT, UPON EXPARTE APPLICATION, PLAINTIFF MAY GET JUDGMENT FOR POSSESSION

JUDGE PRO TEM:IRVING PFEFFER; CLERK:BARBARA MORRIS;REPORTER:TINA PAXTON (218)
